symptoms.symptom_name: Postural dizziness/near-syncope on standing, relieved by lying down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptoms strictly triggered by standing and relieved by recumbency are the defining feature of orthostatic hypotension and point to an autonomic mechanism.
Arrhythmic syncope is typically not strictly postural and often occurs without positional trigger; a purely postural pattern argues against it though does not exclude it.
Positional near-syncope is not a feature of uncomplicated gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic blood-pressure drop (110/70 supine to 85/55 standing) with limited HR compensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained systolic fall of ~25 mmHg on standing with a modest heart-rate rise relative to the magnitude of the drop is characteristic of neurogenic orthostatic hypotension. Clinician confirmation under standardised conditions is required; the home readings are unverified.
Hypovolaemia also produces an orthostatic drop, usually with a more pronounced compensatory tachycardia; a contributing role here is plausible given reduced intake.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea, early satiety and bloating limiting oral fluid intake (GI dysmotility)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic failure impairs GI motility, producing early satiety, bloating and nausea; the combination of autonomic GI and cardiovascular features supports a common autonomic cause.
Inability to maintain intake precipitates and worsens volume depletion, aggravating the orthostatic drop.
Nausea and bloating overlap with gastroenteritis but are non-specific and, without diarrhoea or fever, do not favour it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known recurrent autonomic/nerve condition with prior similar flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, specialist-managed autonomic condition with prior similar episodes strongly raises the prior probability that this is another flare of the same process rather than a new acute illness.
The absence of diarrhoea and fever substantially reduces the likelihood of an acute infective gastroenteritis as the primary cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, no palpitations, no non-postural collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain, palpitations and non-postural collapse reduces the likelihood of a primary arrhythmic cause, though ECG and examination are still required to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac auscultation — regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal examination lowers suspicion for structural or valvular cardiac causes but does not exclude an intermittent arrhythmia; requires clinician auscultation and cannot be assessed via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended but soft abdomen with sluggish bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension with sluggish bowel sounds is consistent with autonomic GI dysmotility rather than an acute surgical or infective abdomen; requires clinician examination and cannot be assessed via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Moist mucous membranes and adequate capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Moist mucous membranes and adequate capillary refill argue against severe clinical dehydration as the sole cause, supporting a primary autonomic mechanism; a clinician finding, not obtainable via chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of strictly postural near-syncope, an orthostatic blood-pressure drop with limited heart-rate compensation, autonomic GI dysmotility limiting hydration, and a known recurrent autonomic condition together indicate an autonomic failure flare rather than an isolated GI illness or simple dehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinician-measured lying and standing blood pressure and heart rate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standardised orthostatic vitals confirm the magnitude of the postural drop and the degree of heart-rate compensation, distinguishing neurogenic from hypovolaemic orthostatic hypotension. Home readings are unverified and must be confirmed in person.
Required to exclude an arrhythmic/conduction cause of syncope in an older adult; cannot be performed via telehealth and forms part of urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods — full blood count, urea/electrolytes/creatinine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for volume depletion, electrolyte disturbance and acute kidney injury from reduced intake, which guide rehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Autoimmune autonomic serology (e.g. ganglionic AChR antibodies) and tilt-table testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the underlying antibody-mediated aetiology; appropriate for specialist neurology follow-up, not the acute telehealth encounter — recommending these as the immediate priority is a distraction from same-day stabilisation.
